1009.reason: [Off-by-one error: when i equals string.length()-1, the last character is still available but max() returns true, causing the loop to terminate prematurely. For an empty string, string.length()-1 is -1, so i >= -1 is true from the start, causing the program to skip processing entirely and produce no output.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Off-by-one error: when i equals string.length()-1, the last character is still available but max() returns true, causing the loop to terminate prematurely. For an empty string, string.length()-1 is -1, so i >= -1 is true from the start, causing the program to skip processing entirely and produce no output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition causes the loop to terminate before processing the last character if it is not a newline, or prematurely stops processing. It should be `i >= string.length()` to ensure all characters are processed by `sort.work()`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `i >= string.length()-1` causes the loop in State1 to terminate one character early. It should be `i >= string.length()` to ensure all characters, including the last one (like the final '}'), are processed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition stops processing one character too early. When the input ends with '}', the index i reaches string.length()-1 but the final character hasn't been processed yet, causing the last token (RBRACE) to be skipped. It should be i >= string.length() instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition causes premature termination: when i == string.length() - 1, it returns true, so the last character (at index string.length()-1) is never processed. It should be `i >= string.length()`]
